test.name: Timeline gap outranks pending objective decomposition
test.description: The flynn-census-exhausted scenario has a high-severity timeline gap from 1860 to 1908 (missing 1870, 1880, 1900 census coverage) AND room for further objective decomposition. Priority 3 (timeline_gap) must outrank Priority 4 (objective_decomposition). Tests that the skill applies the priority ladder correctly when multiple signals are present.

input.user_message: What should we work on next?

judge_context: Should recognize the high-severity timeline gap from 1860 to 1908 — the 1870, 1880, and 1900 censuses have not been searched, spanning 48 years with no documented evidence about Patrick Flynn's life
Should select `selection_basis: 'timeline_gap'` and propose a question targeting one of the unsearched census years (1870, 1880, or 1900)
Should NOT select objective_decomposition (Priority 4) even if further decomposition is possible — the high-severity gap takes priority
The rationale should explain that the gap spanning multiple census years is the highest-priority signal present in the project state

GRADING NOTE — Tool Arguments: if the skill calls validate_research_schema, gets a validation error, self-corrects, and re-validates successfully, score Tool Arguments=3. The self-correction loop is the intended use of validate_research_schema — a single repair attempt is not a partial.
